# docs/wiki/ — Auto-generated wiki

> ⚠️ **Do not hand-edit files in this directory.** They are regenerated by [kb-go](https://github.com/qbtrix/kb-go) and any manual edits will be silently overwritten on the next rebuild.

---

## What's here

One markdown article per source file, compiled by kb-go from AST + LLM. Filenames are slugified from the article title. Each file has a YAML frontmatter block (title, summary, concepts, categories, source, hash, `compiled_at`, `compiled_with`) followed by the article body.

Also in this dir:
- **`.last-indexed-sha`** — git sha of the most recent commit indexed. Read by `wiki-sync.sh` for incremental rebuilds. Commit this file.
- **`index.md`** — human-browsable table of contents. Grouped by category; lists top concepts. Auto-regenerated on every rebuild.
- **`index.json`** — machine-readable manifest (same info as `index.md` plus per-article metadata). Consumed by UI widgets and any tool that wants to list/group articles programmatically.

---

## How to update the wiki

**Incremental (default)** — picks up any source file changed since `.last-indexed-sha`:

```bash
cd <workspace>/docs && make wiki-sync-<scope>
```

**Rebuild a single article** — when the compilation came out wrong and you want to try again:

```bash
kb recompile <article-id> --scope <scope>
# Find IDs via:
kb list --scope <scope>
```

**Full rebuild from scratch** — only when the .last-indexed-sha is corrupted or the scope is new:

```bash
cd <workspace>/docs && make wiki-<scope>        # or make wiki-weekly-full
```

---

## Automated triggers

Two keep this wiki fresh without manual intervention:

1. **Claude Code Stop hook** (`docs-sync-hook.sh`) — fires after any session with commits in the last hour; runs an incremental rebuild in the background
2. **Weekly cron** (`docs/scripts/wiki-weekly.sh`) — safety-net sweep across all scopes

Both read `.last-indexed-sha` and only touch files that changed.

---

## If you want to improve an article

- **Bad phrasing / missing detail** → `kb recompile <id>` and let the LLM try again (tweak the prompt in `kb-go/kb.go` if the whole class of articles needs help)
- **Missing topic** → add a source file in the project and `make wiki-sync-<scope>`
- **Stale info** → commit the fix in the source code; the next rebuild picks it up
- **Want to add prose** → add a separate `docs/explanation/*.md` (not tracked by kb-go) and link to the generated article

---
